description: phy_write_register issues __sync_synchronize() between the CTRL and DATA writes to enforce bus-level ordering. The two debug-register pairs have no such requirement (single internal latch). Unifying onto a shared writer that drops the barrier re-introduces an intermittent PHY initialisation hang on cold boot.

findings.rationale: phy_write_register has an extra __sync_synchronize() call between two MMIO writes; the other two writers go straight ctrl-then-data. The barrier is load-bearing on the PHY init path — store buffers and compiler reordering both produce visible DATA-before-CTRL traffic without it.
